decision: Accepted the snapshot-compare workaround in onSave for the sticky settingsStore.error (treat save as failed only when the post-await error is non-null AND differs from the pre-await snapshot), accepting the narrow residual that two consecutive identical-message save failures render SUCCESS on the second; recorded the real fix as a deferred store-contract follow-up rather than fixing it now.
alternative_not_taken: Fixing it correctly at the source by having SettingsStore.persist() clear error to null on a successful save (or expose a per-attempt error token).
rationale: The correct fix mutates SettingsStore, which the approved Phase 2 brief explicitly walls off (no store error-contract change). The residual is the opposite direction and far rarer than the original always-false-FAILED bug it replaces, and the view cannot honestly distinguish a freshly re-set identical error from a stale one without a store signal it is forbidden to add. Staying inside the wall and deferring the store change is the honest scoped choice.
